required_preferences.fact: Plans frequent in-person conversations and activities with her sister in Ann Arbor, replacing scheduled Sunday evening catch-up calls while sharing caregiving duties

supersedes.fact: Schedules all catch-up calls with out-of-state sisters for Sunday evenings — never does impromptu calls

required_preferences.fact: Pauses all daily walks and outdoor family strolls, substituting with slow indoor movement and gentle activities, strictly avoiding outdoor walks on doctor's orders while wearing the heart monitor

supersedes.fact: Shifts daily 30-minute walks to the early evening with her son and dog to coincide with her husband's evening work meetings — no longer avoids evening walks if family schedule requires it

required_preferences.fact: Schedules weekly video calls with her husband and son to maintain family connection during her semester abroad
Reserves a dedicated workspace in her home for her sister, ensuring quiet hours during weekday afternoons so both can focus on remote work responsibilities
Carefully chooses only loose, comfortable clothing that accommodates electrode placement and avoids high-neck or tight tops while wearing the heart monitor
Checks news headlines only with her first cup of coffee — avoids reading news in the evening

persona: A mid-career sociologist with multiple published journal articles
